sense.definition: extremely pleasing to the mind or senses

examples.x: He painted a beautiful portrait of his sister.
The sunset over the ocean was absolutely beautiful.
She wore a beautiful dress to the party.
The actress is known for her beautiful smile.
The bride looked beautiful as she walked down the aisle.

sense.definition: exceptionally beautiful or splendid, often inspiring awe or admiration

examples.x: The glorious garden was filled with blooming flowers of every color imaginable.
His glorious singing voice echoed through the concert hall, captivating everyone in attendance.
The glorious display of fireworks lit up the night sky with bursts of color and sparkle.
The glorious view from the mountaintop stretched for miles, showcasing the breathtaking landscape below.
The glorious architecture of the cathedral stood as a testament to the skill and craftsmanship of its builders.

sense.definition: very beautiful, attractive, or impressive

examples.x: The stunning landscape of the countryside was captured in the artist's painting.
She wore a stunning dress that caught everyone’s attention.
The view from the mountaintop was absolutely stunning.
His performance was so stunning that it left the audience speechless.
The movie’s special effects were so stunning that they felt almost real.

sense.definition: incredibly impressive or beautiful, often leaving one feeling amazed

examples.x: Standing on the edge of the cliff, we were greeted by a breathtaking panorama of the vast ocean stretching out before us.
The breathtaking speed of the roller coaster left riders exhilarated and breathless.

sense.definition: extremely attractive and beautiful

examples.x: She looked absolutely gorgeous in her evening gown.
The sunset over the ocean was truly gorgeous, with vibrant hues of pink and orange.
He thought his new car was absolutely gorgeous, with its sleek design and polished finish.
The bride was radiant and gorgeous on her wedding day.
The mountain landscape was absolutely gorgeous, with majestic peaks and lush valleys.

sense.definition: exceptionally impressive and beautiful, often bringing joy or admiration

examples.x: The splendid sunset painted the sky in hues of orange and purple.
Her splendid gown sparkled under the lights as she entered the room.
The splendid architecture of the cathedral drew visitors from far and wide.
The splendid performance of the orchestra filled the concert hall with music.
The splendid view from the mountaintop took their breath away.

sense.definition: delightful due to qualities of beauty, suitability, or perfection
having a refined and graceful appearance or style

examples.x: She wore an elegant gown to the gala, turning heads with her timeless beauty.
The bride looked stunning in her elegant wedding dress, radiating grace and charm as she walked down the aisle.
The dining room was decorated with elegant furnishings, creating an atmosphere of sophistication and luxury.
The bride's hairstyle was simple yet elegant, with cascading curls framing her face in soft waves.
The garden was filled with elegant roses, their petals delicately unfurling in the morning sunlight.

sense.definition: exceptionally elegant and innocent

examples.x: Her angelic smile had a way of brightening up everyone's day.
The puppy’s angelic behavior won over even the most skeptical of pet owners.
She had an angelic demeanor, always speaking softly and with genuine kindness.
Her angelic behavior toward the elderly neighbors earned her admiration from the entire community.
His angelic manner of listening intently and offering support made him a cherished friend.

sense.definition: extremely attractive and pleasing
having aesthetic appeal, visual attractiveness, or creative beauty
(literary) beautiful and pleasant to the sight

examples.x: The garden was filled with beauteous flowers, their vibrant colors and delicate petals creating a stunning display.
Her beauteous smile lit up the room, captivating everyone around her.
The beauteous sunset painted the sky in shades of pink, orange, and purple, leaving onlookers in awe.
The beauteous melody of the violin echoed through the concert hall, enchanting the audience.
They marveled at the beauteous architecture of the ancient cathedral, admiring its intricate details and grandeur.

sense.definition: mesmerizing, or captivatingly beautiful
extremely delicate, light, as if it belongs to a heavenly realm

examples.x: The ethereal music of the flute floated through the room, creating a sense of calm.
She wore a gown made of ethereal fabric that seemed to shimmer in the moonlight.
The ethereal mist enveloped the forest, lending an otherworldly atmosphere.
The ethereal scent of jasmine filled the garden, transporting visitors to a dreamlike state.
The cloud formation was so delicate and fluffy that it appeared almost ethereal in the sky.

sense.definition: indescribable or beyond words, often used to describe beauty or aesthetic experiences
impeccably beautiful or ideal in appearance, like a perfectly composed photograph
having exceptional beauty or excellence

examples.x: The pianist's performance of the concerto was sublime, leaving the audience in awe of her talent.
As the sun set over the horizon, painting the sky in hues of pink and gold, the view from the mountaintop was truly sublime.
The sublime craftsmanship of the sculpture made it a masterpiece of art.
Watching the ballet performance, with its graceful movements and captivating music, was an experience of sublime artistry.
The sublime tranquility of the forest was a welcome escape from the hustle and bustle of city life.

sense.definition: inspiring a feeling of wonder or amazement

examples.x: The view from the mountaintop was truly wondrous, with sweeping vistas of untouched wilderness.
The magician performed wondrous tricks that left the audience in awe.
The child's imagination was filled with wondrous stories of faraway lands and magical creatures.
The starry sky on a clear night is a wondrous sight to behold.
The wondrous discovery of a new species in the rainforest excited scientists around the world.

sense.definition: a famous person who is considered to be sexually attractive by many people
someone who is sexually appealing
a sexually attractive or pleasing person
someone or something that is visually attractive but may not have much substance or depth

examples.x: The movie was filled with special effects and eye candy, but the plot was weak.
He’s often dismissed as just eye candy, but he’s a talented actor as well.
The magazine was filled with images of eye candy models in designer clothes.
The sports car wasn’t very practical, but it was certainly eye candy.
Her Instagram is full of eye candy photos of exotic locations and beautiful scenery.

sense.definition: (of a person) physically attractive in a way that draws attention

examples.x: She exudes confidence and allure, making her undeniably sexy.
His chiseled features and magnetic personality make him incredibly sexy to many.
Despite her age, she maintains a youthful allure that many find sexy.
His confident swagger and charismatic smile make him incredibly sexy.

sense.definition: having qualities that make one attractive or worth wanting

examples.x: His charming personality makes him a highly desirable partner.
She is considered desirable because of her intelligence and kindness.
In the dating scene, being desirable often includes confidence and good communication skills.
A desirable person often knows how to engage others with interesting conversation.
The combination of kindness and charisma makes her one of the most desirable individuals at the event.

sense.definition: having an attractive and irresistible quality that captivates others

examples.x: Her seductive smile and flirtatious demeanor made it hard for him to focus on anything else.
The seductive aroma of perfume lingered in the air, drawing him closer.
Her seductive gaze held his attention, leaving him spellbound.
The seductive curves of the sports car caught his eye as it sped past.
The seductive allure of the tropical beach paradise beckoned him to escape reality and unwind.

sense.definition: sexually attractive or desirable

examples.x: She found herself drawn to him because she thought he was hot.
In the movie, the main character is depicted as a hot and desirable bachelor.
The romance novel described the male protagonist as incredibly hot, driving the female characters wild with desire.
Despite their attempts to hide their feelings, it was clear they both found each other hot.
He was known around school as the hot guy everyone had a crush on.

sense.definition: having features or characteristics that are pleasing

examples.x: Her confident and friendly personality makes her very attractive to others.
She wears an attractive outfit that catches everyone's attention at the party.
The charismatic singer has an attractive voice that captivates the audience.
The handsome actor's attractive smile made him a heartthrob for many fans.
The professor is not only knowledgeable but also has an attractive way of presenting complex ideas.

sense.definition: attractive and good-looking

examples.x: She wore a cute, colorful dress to the party.
The cartoon characters in the movie were cute.
The cute boy had curly hair that framed his face.
The cute girl had big, expressive eyes and a button nose.
The little girl's cute giggle brightened everyone's day.

sense.definition: sexually attractive and good-looking
having an attractive, curvy figure

examples.x: She walked in wearing a form-fitting dress, and everyone noticed her juicy curves.
That model has a juicy body that’s hard to miss on the runway.
He couldn’t take his eyes off her juicy figure when she walked by.
The dancer’s moves were as smooth as her juicy physique.

sense.definition: sexually attractive and very seductive

examples.x: The model's luscious hair cascaded down her back, drawing attention to her stunning features.
Her luscious lips were painted a vibrant red, drawing attention to her smile.
His luscious eyes were a deep brown, full of warmth and kindness.
She walked into the room with a luscious confidence that caught everyone’s eye.
The actress was known for her luscious charm, captivating the audience with every scene.

sense.definition: sexually attractive or alluring
visually pleasing in a charming way

examples.x: The girl's pretty eyes reflect her kindness and warmth.
The little girl had a pretty smile that melted hearts.
The woman had a pretty and delicate voice that was pleasant to listen to.
With her pretty eyes and friendly manner, she makes friends easily.

sense.definition: attractive in appearance, particularly in terms of facial features or overall demeanor
dazzling, radiant, or magnificent in appearance

examples.x: The bride looked resplendent in her intricately designed wedding gown, sparkling under the soft lights.
The garden was resplendent with vibrant flowers, creating a breathtaking tapestry of colors.
The monarch appeared resplendent in his royal regalia, with jewels and gold accentuating his majestic presence.
As the sun set, the sky turned resplendent with hues of orange, pink, and purple.
The ballroom was resplendent with crystal chandeliers, luxurious drapes, and beautifully arranged tables.

sense.definition: truly outstanding, remarkable, exceptional, or attractive
attractive and lovely
very beautiful or attractive

examples.x: He had a lovely singing voice that captivated the audience.
He was a lovely person inside and out, always helping others without expecting anything in return.
She had a lovely smile that brightened up the room.
The actress was praised for her lovely and natural acting skills.
The little girl had a lovely personality and was always kind to others.

sense.definition: pleasing and likely to arouse interest or desire
visually striking or captivating

examples.x: The eye-catching mural on the building drew in pedestrians from across the street.
She wore an eye-catching red dress that made her stand out at the event.
The advertisement featured eye-catching graphics that immediately captured the audience's interest.
His eye-catching hairstyle was the talk of the party, inspiring many to try something similar.
The eye-catching packaging of the product helped it fly off the shelves.

sense.definition: attractive and pleasant to the sight
incredibly cute or charming, often causing feelings of affection, delight, or admiration

examples.x: The adorable baby giggled and cooed, bringing joy to everyone around.
The adorable kitten curled up in a ball and purred contentedly in its owner's lap.
The adorable couple held hands and walked along the beach, lost in their love.
The adorable little ducklings followed their mother closely as they waddled by the pond.
The adorable plush toys lined the shelves, tempting children and adults alike.

sense.definition: so interesting that it holds your attention completely

examples.x: The book was both captivating and compulsive, drawing readers in from the first page.

sense.definition: charmingly attractive or enticing
relating to the enjoyment or appreciation of beauty or art, especially visual art

examples.x: She has a great aesthetic sense when it comes to interior design.
She believed the sculpture's meaning should be secondary to its aesthetic form and composition.
Their aesthetic priorities influenced the use of neutral palettes and organic materials in the redesign.
The artist’s work was praised for its unique aesthetic quality and emotional depth.
Her blog is dedicated to exploring the aesthetic aspects of contemporary architecture.

sense.definition: (of a person) having qualities that are highly attractive

examples.x: The actor's delectable smile captivated the audience throughout the film.
She had a delectable charm that made everyone want to be around her.
His delectable looks made him the center of attention at the party.
As she walked by, the delectable woman caught the eye of every passerby.
The delectable celebrity turned heads as she posed for the cameras.

sense.definition: appealing in a way that arouses interest or desire

examples.x: The enticing aroma of freshly baked bread drew customers into the bakery.
The enticing offer of a free trial encouraged many people to sign up for the service.
The enticing display of products in the window caught the attention of shoppers passing by.
The enticing prospect of a tropical vacation motivated them to start saving money.
The enticing sale prices persuaded shoppers to buy more than they had planned.

sense.definition: attractive in a way that catches the eye

examples.x: She wore a fetching smile that brightened the room wherever she went.
He complimented her on her fetching outfit, admiring how it accentuated her figure.

sense.definition: improving or emphasizing someone's good features, making them appear more attractive
stylish, attractive, and often associated with luxury or sophistication

examples.x: She looked absolutely glamorous in her evening gown and sparkling jewelry.
The glamorous party was filled with celebrities and VIP guests.
Her glamorous lifestyle included jet-setting to exotic destinations and attending exclusive events.
The glamorous decor of the hotel lobby exuded an air of opulence and grandeur.
His glamorous sports car turned heads as he drove through the city streets.

sense.definition: aesthetically pleasing, attractive, or appealing
strong, attractive, and captivating quality that draws attention and creates an allure
a person or an animal that is attractive in a sweet or adorable way

examples.x: That puppy is such a little cutie with its big eyes and floppy ears!
You're a real cutie in that outfit today!
That baby at the park was such a cutie, everyone couldn't stop smiling.
My grandma still calls me her little cutie, even though I'm an adult.

sense.definition: an excellent or beautiful person
a very attractive or engaging person or thing
